metadata.title: Predicted Neural Responses for Musical Traditions
basis: Bidelman & Krishnan (2009) - Brainstem FFR correlates with consonance
Blood et al. (1999) - Dissonance/consonance cortical mapping
Sachs et al. (2008) - Midbrain reward response to consonance
Trainor et al. (2002) - Consonance preference emergence
metadata.note: These are computational PREDICTIONS based on published data, not measured responses. Testable with EEG/fMRI.

prediction_1_neural_similarity.statement: Traditions at similar dial positions should produce similar EEG/fMRI responses, regardless of geographic origin
prediction_1_neural_similarity.testable_with: Cross-cultural EEG study: play music from each tradition to naive listeners, measure FFR + cortical ERP

closest_pairs.prediction: EEG responses to Arabic and Turkish music should be similar
EEG responses to Hindustani and Arabic music should be similar
EEG responses to Hindustani and West African music should be similar
EEG responses to Javanese and Balinese music should be similar
EEG responses to Hindustani and Turkish music should be similar

most_distant_pairs.prediction: EEG responses to Balinese and Chinese music should be distinct
EEG responses to Balinese and Western ET music should be distinct
EEG responses to Carnatic and Chinese music should be distinct
EEG responses to Gagaku and Chinese music should be distinct
EEG responses to Gagaku and Western ET music should be distinct

prediction_2_adaptation_cycle.statement: Music in Phase 4 (Saturation) should show fastest neural adaptation (habituation), Phase 1 (Discovery) slowest
prediction_2_adaptation_cycle.mechanism: Familiarity speeds up predictive coding — the brain's prediction error drops faster
prediction_2_adaptation_cycle.testable_with: Long-duration EEG: measure N1/P2 habituation rates for novel vs. familiar musical styles

prediction_3_consonance_universal.statement: Brainstem FFR response to consonance should be similar across all listeners (culturally invariant), but cortical pleasantness ratings should vary by cultural background
prediction_3_consonance_universal.basis: FFR is subcortical (pre-conscious), pleasantness involves cortical + limbic (culturally shaped)
prediction_3_consonance_universal.testable_with: Compare FFR amplitude (brainstem) vs. subjective pleasantness ratings across listeners from different musical traditions

prediction_4_reward_prediction.statement: Music near the listener's cultural tradition should activate nucleus accumbens more than equally consonant unfamiliar music
prediction_4_reward_prediction.mechanism: Reward combines acoustic consonance with cultural familiarity (statistical learning)
prediction_4_reward_prediction.testable_with: fMRI with listeners from 3+ traditions hearing each tradition's music

heatmap_data.description: Cortical activation predicted at I_spectral=2.0 slice across I_vert x I_horiz
